I don't think that's right. When bio_for_each_segment_all doesn't exist, bio_for_each_segment is used instead, which does need "struct bvec_iter iter".

Patch Set 1:

20478 breaks client build against kernel 4.5:
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.c: In function ‘do_bio_lustrebacked’:
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.c:229: error: incompatible types when assigning to type ‘struct bvec_iter’ from type ‘int’
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.c:229: error: incompatible types when assigning to type ‘struct bio_vec’ from type ‘struct bio_vec *’
cc1: warnings being treated as errors
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.c:229: error: left-hand operand of comma expression has no effect
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.c:229: error: invalid operands to binary < (have ‘struct bvec_iter’ and ‘int’)
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.c:229: error: wrong type argument to increment
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.c:229: error: wrong type argument to increment
/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.c:229: error: left-hand operand of comma expression has no effect
make[6]: *** [/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite/lloop.o] Error 1
make[5]: *** [/root/lustre-release/lustre/llite] Error 2
make[4]: *** [/root/lustre-release/lustre] Error 2
make[3]: *** [_module_/root/lustre-release] Error 2
make[3]: Leaving directory `/usr/src/kernels/4.5.3-raijin.el6.x86_64'
make[2]: *** [modules] Error 2
make[2]: Leaving directory `/root/lustre-release'
make[1]: *** [all-recursive] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory `/root/lustre-release'
make: *** [all] Error 2

#define bio_for_each_segment_all(bv, bio, it) bio_for_each_segment(bv, bio, it)
will only work if the two symbols are expecting the same type of bv and it.

I think we should use both #ifdef bio_for_each_segment_all and #ifdef HAVE_BVEC_ITER to determine which symbol to use here

Their has to be a clearer way to handle this.

Patch Set 2:

Okay I did some looking at the git history of the linux kernel. Since the introduction of bio_for_each_segment_all() for the 3.10 kernels it always has taken a integer for its last parameter. As for bio_for_each_segment() it took a integer parameter until the 3.14 kernel. Then it started taking the bvec_iter parameter instead. This means bio_for_each_segment_all() added to lustre to handle 3.9 or earlier kernels should work unless the distro does something really stupid. By stupid I mean they would have to back port all the new bvec_iter infrastructure which I doubt they will since that would be a lot of useless work.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

Why. The changes for bvec_iter happened with 3.14+ kernels and  bio_for_each_segment_all was introduced in 3.9+ kernels. So the backwards compact for bio_for_each_segment_all introduced only matters for 3.8 or earlier kernels which will never have bvec_iter. If you are hard core about bvec_iter support then it should be handled completely in lustre_compact.h.

Patch Set 2:

Why. The changes for bvec_iter happened with 3.14+ kernels and  bio_for_each_segment_all was introduced in 3.9+ kernels. So the backwards compact for bio_for_each_segment_all introduced only matters for 3.8 or earlier kernels which will never have bvec_iter. If you are hard core about bvec_iter support then it should be handled completely in lustre_compact.h.

(1 comment)

Andreas the default loop device only supported direct I/O starting 4.6 kernels so we can't delete it for older kernels. Unless you want to abandon support completely?

Patch Set 3:

James, I suspect that even with older kernels this isn't working very well since it was moved over to CLIO.

It does appear that sanity.sh test_68a is still being run, so I guess lloop is still working on master.  It might be useful to see how the loop driver is doing compared to lloop on older kernels.

Patch Set 3:

Do you mind if we still land this so Ubuntu16 platforms can build. I can investigate loop vs lloop performance on old kernels but that would take time. It would be nice to support Ubuntu16 out of the box for the 2.9 release.

commit.subject: LU-8056 lloop: fix bio_for_each_segment_all for newer kernels
commit.message:
LU-8056 lloop: fix bio_for_each_segment_all for newer kernels

Lustre patch http://review.whamcloud.com/20478 back ported
bio_for_each_segment_all from newer kernels but support
for newer kernels was done incorrectly. Update the code to
work with newer kernels.

Change-Id: I6a926320f80113169a13d2319190721c83d58b1d
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/20619
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Li Dongyang <dongyang.li@anu.edu.au>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
